subject: [DCAE] Include additional SBOM flags

Patch Set 1: Code-Review-2

The SBOM generator runs by default in the $WORKSPACE. 

Please run this locally in your machine so that you are able to see the error message with the dependencies.

Patch Set 1:

> Patch Set 1: Code-Review-2
> 
> The SBOM generator runs by default in the $WORKSPACE. 
> 
> Please run this locally in your machine so that you are able to see the error message with the dependencies.

I verified with local run - this update is more of workaround to address the SBOM executable issues (https://github.com/opensbom-generator/spdx-sbom-generator/issues/227, emailed on this on Friday). 

We need to change LF wrapper update to use the -p path as override to copy the executable itself. This specific change is not required if LF wrapper can use $WORKSPACE as default to copy the binary (when -p path override is not specified).

Patch Set 1:

Sorry,, I am little bit confused.. so,, you were able to test this locally and you are no longer getting the transitive dependencies issues. 
I tested your change and it is the same result:
https://jenkins.onap.org/job/dcaegen2-collectors-ves-maven-stage-master/1015/

I overwrote the job to use -p $WORKSPACE but it didn't had an effect since this job is already using the $WORKSPACE path as default:

Patch Set 1:

> Patch Set 1:
> 
> Sorry,, I am little bit confused.. so,, you were able to test this locally and you are no longer getting the transitive dependencies issues. 
> I tested your change and it is the same result:
> https://jenkins.onap.org/job/dcaegen2-collectors-ves-maven-stage-master/1015/
> 
> I overwrote the job to use -p $WORKSPACE but it didn't had an effect since this job is already using the $WORKSPACE path as default:

Hey Jess - To avoid the transitive dependency error, we need to copy the spdx-sbom-generator binary to -p specified path before executing it. This is a required as the path parameter is not being read by SBOM binary currently; copying the binary to target/required path gets pass the transitive dependency error. 

This workaround may be required under SBOM generator community fixes the issue.

Majority of ONAP project would have $WORKSPACE as root, but that is not true for all cases (e.g dcaegen2/platform or dcaegen2/services) where we can override the -p to required path for those subcomponent. 

So once the LF wrapper script -“sbom-generator.sh” is updated to copy the binary to specified path (and switch to this directory) before invoking it - we should be good.

Patch Set 1:

> Patch Set 1:
> 
> > Patch Set 1:
> > 
> > Sorry,, I am little bit confused.. so,, you were able to test this locally and you are no longer getting the transitive dependencies issues. 
> > I tested your change and it is the same result:
> > https://jenkins.onap.org/job/dcaegen2-collectors-ves-maven-stage-master/1015/
> > 
> > I overwrote the job to use -p $WORKSPACE but it didn't had an effect since this job is already using the $WORKSPACE path as default:
> 
> Hey Jess - To avoid the transitive dependency error, we need to copy the spdx-sbom-generator binary to -p specified path before executing it. This is a required as the path parameter is not being read by SBOM binary currently; copying the binary to target/required path gets pass the transitive dependency error. 
> 
> This workaround may be required under SBOM generator community fixes the issue.
> 
> Majority of ONAP project would have $WORKSPACE as root, but that is not true for all cases (e.g dcaegen2/platform or dcaegen2/services) where we can override the -p to required path for those subcomponent. 
> 
> So once the LF wrapper script -“sbom-generator.sh” is updated to copy the binary to specified path (and switch to this directory) before invoking it - we should be good.

As discussed via email (offline), this change is not required when below rules are instrumented in wrapper script by LF member

- If no path flag is given, then copy and use the spdx binary in the $WORKSPACE
- If path flag is given, then copy and use the spdx binary in the "path" given

As ves repo uses default (i.e $WORKSPACE), path override is not required. 

Jess - Let us know once you are able to verify with script changes.
